title: impatiens roylei var. moschata
intro: Impatiens roylei var. moschata, also known as the sweet-scented balsam, is a plant species that belongs to the Impatiens genus. It is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan region, prized for its medicinal properties and sweet fragrance. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots and leaves of Impatiens roylei var. moschata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
